title: Job Openings at Tetra Maritime
content:
Tetra Maritime is a leading Nigerian ship owner and operator, providing comprehensive and tailor-made solutions across the oil and gas supply chain, including upstream, midstream, and downstream.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Business Management Analyst / Associate

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dynamic Business Management professional to support executive operations and strategic initiatives.
	This role is ideal for an ambitious early-career professional with exceptional organization, process-management, and problem-solving skills, paired with excellent attention to detail and communication abilities.
	You will work closely with senior leadership, contributing to high-level decision-making, operational efficiency, and cross-functional projects, providing analytical and operational support to executive leadership. 


Roles and Responsibilities


	Executive Support: The crucial core function is the provision of high-class operational support to executive leadership—at the top of the global organization, including the C-suite—across the full spectrum of executive activities: meeting planning and management, external engagements, project management, communications, and more. Flexibility, agility, and responsiveness to fast-paced and demanding executive itineraries is key. 
	Reporting & Communication: Prepare high-quality reports, presentations, and communications for internal and external stakeholders. 
	Process Optimization: Manage and improve business processes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness. 
	Project Coordination: Coordinate cross-functional projects and ensure timely delivery of outcomes. 


Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Law, Finance, or any field that demonstrates strong analytical and organizational capabilities. 
	We value diverse academic backgrounds and perspectives. 
	Experience: 1 - 3 years of relevant experience in a fast-paced, professional environment (e.g., consulting, finance, legal, corporate operations, or equivalent high-pressure roles). 


Skills:


	Exceptional organizational and process-management skills. 
	Strong problem-solving abilities and analytical thinking. 
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
	High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities. 


Benefits


	This is a unique opportunity to be part of a high-impact team at the heart of the business.
	The role offers exposure to strategic decision-making and the potential for career progression within the organization. 


Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Accounts Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Slots: 2 Openings

Responsibilities


	Maintain cash & bank book, bank reconciliations
	Preparing schedules for tax returns – VAT, Withholding taxes, audit schedules
	Preparation of sales invoices, credit notes, sales summary analysis
	Updating vendor & customer accounts and reconciliations
	Sales, purchase transactions entries in the accounting system
	Bank Payments vouchers preparation
	Inter-company reconciliations
	Documents upkeeping for audits
	Aid during audits – documents readiness, schedules preparation, filing and document ownership
	Visits to bank and tax offices for compliances
	Any other jobs may get assigned as and when need arises.


Requirements


	BSc / MSc in Accounting, Computer knowledge – MS office, accounting software – Tally / Sage / SAP is must
	Work experience in any manufacturing unit / shipping agency.


Additional Information:


	The role is based in our Lagos office, 5 days per week.


Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Corporate Affairs Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	A Documentation Assistant in a Nigerian manning or ship crewing agency is responsible for ensuring that all seafarer?related documents, certifications, and compliance records are accurately processed, verified, updated, and maintained to meet both company standards and international maritime regulatory requirements.
	The role is central to supporting the Crew Operations or Fleet Personnel team by ensuring that each seafarer deployed meets all statutory, client, and flag?state requirements.


Key Responsibilities
Pre?Joining & Compliance Documentation:


	Review the pre?departure checklist and take necessary actions under the Personnel Officer’s (PO’s) guidance.
	Apply for flag?state licenses as instructed by the PO and attach received flag?state documents.
	Re?verify seafarer documents in the crewing software to ensure accuracy and compliance.
	Ensure all pre?joining documents are properly completed and uploaded in the system.
	Coordinate with the PO to ensure briefing forms are fully completed and uploaded.
	Complete and update the pre?departure checklist, then notify the PO for final approval.
	Ensure contract letters are signed and uploaded as provided by shipowners/managers.


Crew Change & Logistics Support:


	Follow up with vendors to arrange working gears/PPE and assist the PO in preparing for crew joining.
	Prepare document packets containing original certificates, flag?state documents, and any other connecting items needed for joining crew.
	Arrange and monitor travel, boarding, and lodging logistics for seafarers.


Post?Joining Documentation:


	Upload all post?joining documents (as required by the post?joining checklist) within 24 hours of the crew’s departure from homeport.
	Upload all appraisals, relief letters, extension letters, or related requests promptly upon receipt.Attach all crew miscellaneous documents in the seafarer’s correspondence section.


Contract & Employment Records Management:


	Prepare transfer, extension, vessel name change, or promotion/increment contracts as required and upload them in the system (seafarer important remark tab).
	Prepare and send sea?service letters and DCE letters for ex?crew members after verifying cargo lists with the PO.


Administrative & Software Updates:


	Ensure all invoices related to crew operations are promptly uploaded in the software.
	Maintain accurate, timely updates of all documentation in the crewing system to support audit readiness.


General Support Duties:


	Provide additional support as assigned by management to ensure smooth crew operations.


Requirements
Qualifications & Required Knowledge:


	Experience: 1 - 4 years in communications, documentation, and people management roles, ideally within global or at least regional organizations.
	Technical Proficiency: Familiarity with communication platforms (e.g., Microsoft 365, SharePoint, Teams).


Core Competencies & Skills
Technical Skills:


	Knowledge of STCW, MLC 2006, and DP/flag?state documentation requirements.
	Strong document verification skills and attention to detail.


Soft Skills:


	Excellent organisation and multitasking ability.
	Strong communication skills (written and verbal).
	Ability to meet strict deadlines, especially during crew change cycles.
	High integrity, with an eye for compliance and accuracy.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "JV Management Consulting - We are passionate about supporting businesses across Africa in order to help them achieve sustainable growth. With a team of experts and a wealth of experience, we specialise in providing innovative management solutions that empower organisations to thrive in today&#39;s dynamic and ever-evolving business landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Videographer

Job Location:&nbsp;Sangotedo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Videographer to join our team in a fast-growing real estate company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for capturing high-quality video content that showcases our properties, brand identity, and marketing campaigns.
	The role requires strong storytelling ability, technical expertise in videography, and the ability to produce engaging visual content that attracts potential clients and investors.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan, shoot, and edit high-quality videos for property listings, promotional campaigns, and social media platforms
	Create compelling visual stories that highlight property features and the company brand
	Operate and maintain video equipment, including cameras, lighting, microphones, and drones where applicable
	Collaborate with the marketing team to develop creative video concepts and content strategies
	Edit and deliver professional video content within project timelines
	Ensure consistency in visual quality and brand representation across all video materials.


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Videographer or Video Content Producer
	Strong proficiency in video editing software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools
	Good understanding of lighting, camera angles, sound recording, and visual composition
	Experience creating marketing or promotional videos (real estate experience is an advantage)
	Creative mindset with strong attention to detail.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;and cover letter to:&nbsp;jvmcrecruitment@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Videographer Job at JV Management Consulting




        
    

        
        JV Management Consulting - We are passionate about supporting businesses across Africa in order to help them achieve sustainable growth. With a team of experts and a wealth of experience, we specialise in providing innovative management solutions that empower organisations to thrive in today's dynamic and ever-evolving business landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Videographer

Job Location: Sangotedo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Videographer to join our team in a fast-growing real estate company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for capturing high-quality video content that showcases our properties, brand identity, and marketing campaigns.
	The role requires strong storytelling ability, technical expertise in videography, and the ability to produce engaging visual content that attracts potential clients and investors.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan, shoot, and edit high-quality videos for property listings, promotional campaigns, and social media platforms
	Create compelling visual stories that highlight property features and the company brand
	Operate and maintain video equipment, including cameras, lighting, microphones, and drones where applicable
	Collaborate with the marketing team to develop creative video concepts and content strategies
	Edit and deliver professional video content within project timelines
	Ensure consistency in visual quality and brand representation across all video materials.


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Videographer or Video Content Producer
	Strong proficiency in video editing software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools
	Good understanding of lighting, camera angles, sound recording, and visual composition
	Experience creating marketing or promotional videos (real estate experience is an advantage)
	Creative mindset with strong attention to detail.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Graphics Designer Job at Velox Real Estate and investment Limited




        
    

        
        Velox Real Estate and investment Limited is a real estate company, specialised in the sales and development of landed properties and we are located in portharcourt, Rivers state, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphics Designer

Job Location: Portharcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities

	Developing property adverts and performing basic video editing or motion graphics for online promotions.
	Mastering industry-standard tools such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, and CorelDRAW to deliver polished results.
	Preparing high-quality files for both professional print production and seamless digital publishing.

title: Recruitment Manager Job at Uni Technology Nigeria Limited
content:
Uni Technology Nigeria Limited, a fintech services company offering funding in a seamless way, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recruitment Manager

Job Location: Ogba / Acme, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Recruitment Manager to design and oversee our company’s hiring.
	Recruitment managers will work closely with our recruiters to manage sourcing, interviewing and employment processes.
	To be successful in this role, recruitment managersshould have a HR academic background and experience screening and evaluating candidates.
	Candidates should also be knowledgeable about labor legislation.
	The ideal candidates is a team leader who is able to make effective decisions quickly.
	Ultimately, recruiting managers will manage our recruitment to ensure we hire qualified employees to meet our company’s current and future needs.


Responsibilities


	Update current and design new recruiting procedures (e.g. job application and onboarding processes)
	Incharge of recruitment for the company across all department.
	Keep track of recruiting metrics (e.g.time-to-hire and cost-per-hire)
	Implement new sourcing methods (e.g. social recruiting and Boolean searches)
	Review recruitment software and suggest the best option for company needs
	Research and choose job advertising options
	Advise hiring managers on interviewing techniques
	Recommend ways to improve our employer brand
	Stay up-to-date on labor legislation and inform recruiters and managers about changes in regulations
	Participate in job fairs and career events
	Build the company’s professional network through relationships with HR professionals, colleges and other partners


Requirements and skills


	BSc in Human Resources Management or Organizational Psychology
	Proven work experience as a Recruiting Manager, Recruitment Consultant or Recruiting Coordinator
	Hands-on experience with Applicant Tracking Systems and HR databases
	Knowledge of labor legislation
	Experience with (phone and in-person) interviews, candidate screening and evaluation
	Familiarity with social media and other professional networks (like GitHub)
	Excellent verbal and written communication and team management skills
	Strong decision-making skills
	Age: 30-45 years.


Salary
N150,000 - N180,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] and copy [email protected]t using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: This is an immediate hiring.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Manager",
  "description": "Alfred and Victoria Associates has become one of the most sought after Human Capital Development Organization in recent times. She is fast becoming the distinguished strategic partner required in all sectors of the economy, and also in the fast growing world of outsourcing which is now in huge demand in all profession. AVA delivers optimal value &amp; Return on Investment (ROI) through Empowerment and continuous Improvement by creating a niche in the development of Human Resources in various sectors of the economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Working Hours: The role requires work from Monday to Saturday, with Saturday being a half-day.
Note: All Public Holidays will also be observed as half-day working days.

Job Summary &amp; Purpose


	The HR Manager works directly with the COO and the Managing Partner to implement and execute strategic and operational initiatives for the firm.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the firm&rsquo;s overall business strategy.
	Develop, recommend and implement personnel policies and procedures;
	Prepares and maintains handbook on policies and procedures.
	Performs benefits administration to include claims resolution, change reporting, approving invoices for payment, annual re-evaluation of policies for costeffectiveness, information activities program and cash flow.
	Develop department goals, objectives and systems.
	Implement and annually update the firm&rsquo;s compensation program;
	Rewrites job descriptions as necessary; conducts annual salary surveys and develops merit pool (salary budget); analyses compensation;
	Monitors the performance evaluation program and revises as necessary.
	Conduct recruitment effort for all required roles;
	Engages with supervisors to screen and interview candidates.
	Conducts reference checking.
	Extends job offers.
	Coordinate new-employee orientations.
	Monitors career-path program and employee relations counselling.
	Conducts exit interviews.
	Ensure planning, monitoring, and appraisal of employee work results by training supervisors to coach and discipline employees; scheduling management conferences with employees; hearing and resolving employee grievances; counselling employees and supervisors.
	Establish and maintains department records and reports.
	Participate in administrative staff meetings and attends other meetings, such as seminars.
	Maintain organisational charts and employee directory.
	Maintain historical human resource records by designing a filing and retrieval system; keeping past and current records.
	Evaluate reports, decisions and results of department initiatives in relation to established goals. Recommends innovative approaches, policies and procedures to effect continual improvements in efficiency of department and services.


Requirements


	Academic Qualification: A relevant bachelor&rsquo;s and/or master&rsquo;s degree
	Professional Qualification: HR Certifications &ndash; CIPM/ SHRM,/GPHRM;
	Experience:&nbsp;Minimum of eight (8) years&rsquo; work experience in human resource management.


Key Skills:


	Very strong people skills
	Comfortable use of MS office;
	Ability to find innovative solution to day-to-day HR problems;
	Proactive in determining HR needs for the firm
	Ability to provide hands on HR support for the firm
	Show adaptability, willingness to learn, and commitment to exceptional delivery;
	Good language skills;
	Good documentation skills;
	Able to multitask effectively;
	Effective time management skills;


Competencies:


	Business Acumen.
	Communication.
	Consultation.
	Critical Evaluation.
	Cultural Awareness.
	HR Expertise.
	Leadership &amp; Navigation.
	Relationship Management.
	Ethical Practice.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to: apply@alfred-victoria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

HR Manager Job at Alfred and Victoria Associates




        
    

        
        Alfred and Victoria Associates has become one of the most sought after Human Capital Development Organization in recent times. She is fast becoming the distinguished strategic partner required in all sectors of the economy, and also in the fast growing world of outsourcing which is now in huge demand in all profession. AVA delivers optimal value & Return on Investment (ROI) through Empowerment and continuous Improvement by creating a niche in the development of Human Resources in various sectors of the economy.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager
Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Working Hours: The role requires work from Monday to Saturday, with Saturday being a half-day.
Note: All Public Holidays will also be observed as half-day working days.

Job Summary & Purpose


	The HR Manager works directly with the COO and the Managing Partner to implement and execute strategic and operational initiatives for the firm.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the firm’s overall business strategy.
	Develop, recommend and implement personnel policies and procedures;
	Prepares and maintains handbook on policies and procedures.
	Performs benefits administration to include claims resolution, change reporting, approving invoices for payment, annual re-evaluation of policies for costeffectiveness, information activities program and cash flow.
	Develop department goals, objectives and systems.
	Implement and annually update the firm’s compensation program;
	Rewrites job descriptions as necessary; conducts annual salary surveys and develops merit pool (salary budget); analyses compensation;
	Monitors the performance evaluation program and revises as necessary.
	Conduct recruitment effort for all required roles;
	Engages with supervisors to screen and interview candidates.
	Conducts reference checking.
	Extends job offers.
	Coordinate new-employee orientations.
	Monitors career-path program and employee relations counselling.
	Conducts exit interviews.
	Ensure planning, monitoring, and appraisal of employee work results by training supervisors to coach and discipline employees; scheduling management conferences with employees; hearing and resolving employee grievances; counselling employees and supervisors.
	Establish and maintains department records and reports.
	Participate in administrative staff meetings and attends other meetings, such as seminars.
	Maintain organisational charts and employee directory.
	Maintain historical human resource records by designing a filing and retrieval system; keeping past and current records.
	Evaluate reports, decisions and results of department initiatives in relation to established goals. Recommends innovative approaches, policies and procedures to effect continual improvements in efficiency of department and services.


Requirements


	Academic Qualification: A relevant bachelor’s and/or master’s degree
	Professional Qualification: HR Certifications – CIPM/ SHRM,/GPHRM;
	Experience: Minimum of eight (8) years’ work experience in human resource management.


Key Skills:


	Very strong people skills
	Comfortable use of MS office;
	Ability to find innovative solution to day-to-day HR problems;
	Proactive in determining HR needs for the firm
	Ability to provide hands on HR support for the firm
	Show adaptability, willingness to learn, and commitment to exceptional delivery;
	Good language skills;
	Good documentation skills;
	Able to multitask effectively;
	Effective time management skills;


Competencies:


	Business Acumen.
	Communication.
	Consultation.
	Critical Evaluation.
	Cultural Awareness.
	HR Expertise.
	Leadership & Navigation.
	Relationship Management.
	Ethical Practice.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
